_comment: Video pool for the daily social autoposter. pick_daily_video.py rotates through `pool`; video_publisher.py posts and then RETIRES each posted clip (removes its entry here and deletes the MP4). Refresh the pool from the factory vault (~/videos/_vault).

pool.caption: PlayStation is phasing out discs and even the EU says it can't stop them. Your disc console is worth up to $380 right now — sell it while discs still matter. purchasingcorp.com
A court just ordered Xbox to restore a hacked account after telling the user to re-buy all his games. Digital can vanish. Hardware is real — and worth up to $310 cash. purchasingcorp.com
Switch 2 hype is everywhere — and your old Switch still pays, up to $180 toward the upgrade. Don't let it collect dust. Quote in 60 seconds at purchasingcorp.com
You can't sell a download. But every device in your house is an asset — the average home has $500+ in unused tech. Own it? Sell it. purchasingcorp.com
Junk drawer audit: old earbuds $40, smartwatch $90, forgotten tablet $150, last phone $250, retired console $430. That drawer is a paycheck. purchasingcorp.com
PC gamers: your old GPU basically buys the new one. Used cards bring up to $680, paid same day. Sell the old card, cover the upgrade — that's the loop. purchasingcorp.com
3 signs it's time to sell your phone: it dies before lunch, storage is always full, and you already know which one you want next. Average payout: $340. purchasingcorp.com
Everyone forgets the iPad — the most undersold device in the house. Used iPads bring up to $260 toward the new one. Check yours in 60 seconds at purchasingcorp.com
That old iPhone in your drawer is worth real cash. Any model, any condition — we paid $635 the same day. Quote in 60 seconds, cash before bedtime. purchasingcorp.com

That golden goal has everyone hosting watch parties — and suddenly shopping. Before you spend, sell: the average home has $500 of unused tech sitting in drawers. Cash before kickoff at purchasingcorp.com
Myth: old tech is worthless. Fact: a 4-year-old phone still brings $250, and older consoles sell every day. Old is an asset. purchasingcorp.com

Courts just ruled game accounts can be inherited — like property. The law is catching up: your tech is an asset. Your hardware can cash out today, up to $430 for a last-gen console. purchasingcorp.com
Physical vs digital, round one: digital resale value is zero, forever. A disc console holds up to $380. Physical wins — cash the win at purchasingcorp.com
Cracked screen, dead battery, three years old — guess what we paid for it. $50? Higher. $100? Higher. $190, for a phone with a shattered screen. Guess yours, then check for real: purchasingcorp.com
Confession: bought a smartwatch in January, wore it six weeks, drawer ever since. Two minutes on the couch → $140 the next day. Your drawer has a confession too. purchasingcorp.com
Camera bag math: body $260, that 50mm you loved $180, flash + extras $80. That's $520 on one closet shelf. Do your own math at purchasingcorp.com
Your old MacBook loses ~$25 in value every month it sits. Sell in July: $480. Wait till October: $400. Same laptop — only the date changed. Lock today's price at purchasingcorp.com
POV: the movers charge by the box and box seven is all old tech you'll never plug in again. Photograph it, quote it, ship it free — box seven just paid $600 toward the move. purchasingcorp.com
Everyone's doing the headphone swap trend — meanwhile two dead pairs of earbuds sit in your drawer worth $90 combined. Do the trend with your good pair, cash out the rest. purchasingcorp.com

Y2K is so back that people are bedazzling digital cameras. That old digicam in your closet just became cool again — working ones bring up to $120. Nostalgia is a market; you're holding inventory. purchasingcorp.com
Gratitude list, tech drawer edition: the old phone $250, the forgotten tablet $150, the watch + earbuds $130. That's $530 of blessings in one drawer. Count yours at purchasingcorp.com
You give this phone 3 hours a day. The last one? Face-down in a drawer earning $0, 24 hours a day. Average payout for a retired phone: $340. Give us 60 seconds of your scroll. purchasingcorp.com
Fintech apps pay 5% interest now. Your junk drawer pays -20% — every device in there loses a fifth of its value yearly. Average balance: $500. Close the worst account you own. purchasingcorp.com
A startup just went 0→10M users in 30 days. In those same 30 days your old console lost $30 in value. The internet moves in 30-day windows — your drawer should too. purchasingcorp.com
AR glasses are everywhere — the next gadget wave is here. The one rule that keeps gadget people rich: one in, one out. The average one-out pays $300 toward the one-in. purchasingcorp.com
Spain: 6 World Cup games, zero goals allowed. Your tech drawer: 6 years, zero dollars produced. One streak is legendary — the other has $500 waiting to get on the board. purchasingcorp.com
Esports World Cup szn — everyone's upgrading their setup. Think roster: new rig is your starting lineup, and the bench (old GPU $680, retired console $430) pays for the starters. purchasingcorp.com
Day in the life, summer edition: 9AM coffee + 60-second quote, noon box shipped free, 6PM $400 hits the account. The drawer paid for the best day of summer. Your turn tomorrow. purchasingcorp.com
